commit.message:
fix: Telegram bot improvements — inspect, dedup, context (#54)

* fix: handle Anthropic tool_use finish_reason in agent loop

The agent loop checked for finish_reason == "tool_calls" (OpenAI
format) but Anthropic returns "tool_use". This caused the loop to
break after round 1 without processing tool results, producing
empty responses ("no response generated").

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add /inspect command to Telegram bot

Shows tool actions, sources, and timing from the last query.
Also captures statuses, sources, and perf_trace events from
the chat SSE stream for inspection.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: prevent duplicate Telegram messages from Claude Code sessions

[NOTIFY] messages were sent during assistant events AND re-extracted
from the result event, causing duplicates. Now skips re-extraction
when notifications were already sent during the session.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: detect imperative follow-ups for conversation context retention

"look in my email", "check my calendar", "search drive for it" etc.
were not recognized as follow-ups, so the previous question's context
was lost. Added regex pattern for imperative verbs + data source nouns.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: add tests for PR review findings; strip PII from /inspect

- Add unit tests for expand_followup_query imperative patterns
- Add tests for orchestrator notification dedup (notifications_sent > 0)
- Move _IMPERATIVE_FOLLOWUP_RE to module level per convention
- Strip tool input args from /inspect output (show tool name only)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
Add 17 database design workshop labs

Part 1 - Relational DB with PostgreSQL (labs 01-08):
single table, one-to-many, many-to-many, one-to-one & normalization,
advanced queries, indexing & performance, views & functions, design challenge.

Part 2 - Alternative DB technologies (labs 09-17):
MongoDB document DB, Neo4j graph DB, TimescaleDB time series,
Redis key-value, Elasticsearch search engine, polyglot persistence capstone.

Each lab is self-contained with docker-compose.yml, init/seed data,
exercises, and Thai-language README with English technical terms.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Pin analytics to exact campaign URNs for data accuracy

Root cause of wrong impressions/spend/CTR: adAnalyticsV2 without
campaign-level filtering returned data from all campaigns the token
can access — not just Spectatr's. Numbers were inflated ~148x.

Fix: linkedin_get_analytics() now accepts campaignUrns[] parameter.
When provided, appends campaigns[0]=...&campaigns[1]=... filters to
the URL — scoping the response to exactly those campaign IDs.

Orchestrator now runs in two phases:
  Phase 1: linkedin_get_campaigns (get exact campaign IDs)
  Phase 2: analytics called with those URNs + creatives + history

Prompt rules added for correct aggregation:
  - SUM daily rows per campaign for impressions/clicks/spend/leads
  - CTR = recalculate from summed clicks/impressions (not avg of CTR field)
  - Frequency = total impressions / total approximateUniqueImpressions
  - WoW delta = real comparison vs prior-week ALL-granularity call
  - Currency noted as INR ₹, cite exact values

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Suppress newlines inside parentheses and add n-body benchmark

Lexer now tracks paren/bracket depth and suppresses newline tokens
when inside () or []. Also suppresses newlines before leading binary
operators (+, -, *, /, %). This enables multi-line function parameters
and expressions without workarounds.

Add n-body gravitational simulation benchmark (50M steps).
Generated Rust code runs at 1.74s, near-identical to native Rust 1.69s.

Add in-process AI player: no separate service needed

- New POST /games/{id}/ai endpoint adds AI opponents directly in the engine
- AI runs as asyncio background tasks — no ed-ai service required
- Heuristic action selection with difficulty-based behavior:
  - Apprentice: mostly random
  - Journeyman: prefers high-point cards, some randomness
  - Master: always picks optimal heuristic move
- Support multiple AI players per game with unique forest-themed names
  (Rugwort, Bramblewick, Thistledew, Mossclaw, etc.)
- AI broadcasts state updates via SSE after each move
- UI "Add AI" button calls engine directly via /api proxy

Glossary letter/category pages omitted locale from generateStaticParams,
causing "Page changed from static to dynamic at runtime" errors in
production. Also unified all hardcoded ['en', 'ja'] locale arrays across
7 page files to use SUPPORTED_LOCALES from @/config, and documented the
pattern in CLAUDE.md.

Fix affordance, data integrity, and criterion violations

- Remove role="link" from benefit and event cards; use semantic stretch-link
  pattern (<a class="card-link"> with ::before overlay, interactive children
  at z-index 1). Removes nested-interactive accessibility issue.
- Align events category list with workflow schema: replace "competition" with
  "workshop". Prevents workshop events from silently missing a filter tab.
- Give remote-only toggle a distinct green-tinted appearance in unpressed
  state, matching the free-only toggle on the main page.
- Add skeleton loading state to events page (was blank on load).
- Add fetch error handling to index.html and events/index.html.
- Add category field to benefit search matching.
- Merge duplicate .card:hover .card-name CSS rules.
- Normalize escapeHtml to escape single quotes across all pages.
- Remove Linear repo field (Linear is not open-source).
- Replace hardcoded years in discover-benefits and discover-events queries
  with a YEAR placeholder; instruct the agent to substitute the current year.
- Convert agent page inline onclick attributes to event delegation.
- Fetch real benefit count from benefits.json for simulation step; removes
  hardcoded "59" that silently diverged from actual data.

commit.message:
feat: suppress repeated blocked signals with GRASS mechanism

When Claude's top buy is blocked by the mandate for 3+ consecutive cycles
for the same reason, the token is suppressed for 2 cycles:

- src/agent/block-tracker.js: new module — tracks per-token consecutive
  block streaks (normalized reason key, 1.5× cycle-interval streak window),
  triggers 2-cycle suppression on 3rd hit, auto-lifts after 2 cycles
- prompts.js: buildContext accepts suppressedTokens set — filters from
  Momentum Leaders, Sustained Movers, New Launch Signals, DexScreener launches;
  adds "## Suppressed Signals" notice so Claude knows why tokens are missing
- reasoning.js: accepts and forwards suppressedTokens to buildContext
- routes/reason.js: ticks suppression counters at cycle start, passes
  suppressedTokens to reason(), parses violations post-cycle to detect
  blocked buys, appends "GRASS suppressed — repeated block: SYMBOL" to
  decision.riskFlags for the reasoning trace

commit.message:
feat: add items comparison, storefront auth, website config, public site modules and fix JSON serialization

- Add items comparison feature (invoicing): Redis cache, polling endpoint, SSE events,
  frontend modal with provider vs system product reconciliation
- Fix JSON serialization for compare DTOs (add snake_case json tags) to resolve
  frontend crash when polling Redis-cached results
- Fix Softpymes client: handle string prices (itemPrice/unitCost) with ParseFloat
- Move storefront pages from (storefront) to (auth) route group
- Add website-config module (backend + frontend) for business site customization
- Add publicsite module (backend + frontend) for public-facing business pages
- Add storefront and website integration types
- Refactor "Tus Integraciones" into dedicated my-integrations module

commit.message:
feat(executor): support CLI-driven runs via remote backend

When Terraform CLI uses the remote backend, the Java API sets
branch = "remote-content" and source = the tar.gz download URL
instead of a git repo. The Go executor was blindly git-cloning
the source URL, which fails since it's a Terrakube API URL.

Fix: mirrors Java SetupWorkspaceImpl.prepareWorkspace():
- If branch == "remote-content" → download tar.gz from source
  URL using a generated API bearer token, extract to temp dir
- Otherwise → existing git clone path (unchanged)

Also implement proper handleConfigVersions in the Go TFE API:
- POST  configuration-versions          → create, return upload-url
- PUT   configuration-versions/<id>     → store tar.gz to storage
- GET   configuration-versions/<id>/terraformContent.tar.gz → serve

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add background re-harvest on startup and version display in get_mcp_server

Startup now fires a non-blocking background refresh that re-harvests tools
from servers whose index is older than 5 minutes, keeping the FTS5 index
fresh without delaying startup. Shutdown awaits any in-flight refresh.

get_mcp_server now displays the downstream server version (from the MCP
handshake) when available.

commit.message:
fix: systematic canonical path normalization in dashboard server

On Windows, Path::canonicalize() prepends the \\?\ extended-path prefix,
causing non-canonical paths (e.g. C:\Users\foo) and canonical paths
(\\?\C:\Users\foo) to compare unequal as HashMap keys.  This was the
root cause of all Windows dashboard 500 errors: known_projects was keyed
by canonical paths at startup but active_project_path held a raw path,
so every get_db_pool lookup missed.

Establish a clear invariant: ALL keys in known_projects and
active_project_path are canonical, normalized once at write time via the
new canonical_path() helper.  Read paths are plain HashMap lookups —
no per-call normalization needed.

Changes:
- Add canonical_path() module-level helper with invariant doc comment
- get_db_pool: &PathBuf → &std::path::Path; canonicalizes lookup key
- add_project: canonicalizes path before HashMap insert
- switch_active_project: uses canonical_path() helper (simplified)
- remove_project: &PathBuf → &std::path::Path; canonicalizes both
  the incoming path and host_project.path before comparison, and uses
  canonical key for HashMap::remove (previously silently returned None)
- DashboardServer::run: computes host_canonical once, reuses for both
  the initial map insert and active_project_path initialization

Fixes 5 Windows dashboard integration tests (test-full / windows-latest).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Refactor: split monolith into internal packages

Split the 2092-line main.go and 2140-line main_test.go into well-bounded
internal packages for readability and testability:

- internal/envutil: file discovery, shell quoting, validation patterns
- internal/template: {{SECRET}} template rendering
- internal/merge: 3-way merge algorithms (pure, no internal deps)
- internal/crypto: AES-GCM encryption, age key wrapping, HMAC-SHA256 MAC
- internal/keyring: OS keyring access, key derivation
- internal/sshkeys: SSH-to-age conversion, ed25519 key discovery
- internal/github: GitHub CLI integration, SSH key fetching
- internal/encfile: encrypted file I/O, encrypt/decrypt, auto-resolve
- internal/cli: all cobra commands, styles, Execute()
- internal/testutil: shared test helpers

Key design change: decryptSecrets and reWrapDataKey now accept privateKey
as a parameter instead of calling getKey() internally, eliminating the
encfile → keyring dependency and making these functions trivially testable.

Tests converted to table-driven where appropriate (merge, template, MAC
tampering). main.go reduced to 6 lines.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
security: second-pass audit — schema, hardcoded values, endpoint hardening

Schema (prisma db push applied):
- Payment, InventoryRestock, ShipmentEvent, DropAlert, SupplierSubmission: add onDelete: Cascade
- InventoryRestock, RepTask, ProductDrop: add updatedAt @updatedAt
- ShipmentEvent: add createdAt @default(now())
- Order: add @@index([placedByRepId]) for rep performance queries

Endpoints:
- /api/bootstrap: remove hardcoded secret from comment, add idempotency guard (409 if admin already exists for another user)
- /api/intake/[id]/cal-booked: add HMAC-SHA256 signature verification (CAL_WEBHOOK_SECRET) + cuid format validation on id param
- /api/inventory: add Clerk auth (was fully public), reduce take from 5000 → 500
- /api/intake: SSRF-guard website URL before scraping, add Retry-After header on 429, add audit event on submission

Env vars:
- lib/email/notifications.ts: ADMIN_EMAIL + FROM now read from env vars (no more hardcoded personal email)
- lib/build/github.ts: GITHUB_OWNER reads from env var (GITHUB_OWNER=adamwolfe2 added to Vercel)
- ADMIN_EMAIL + GITHUB_OWNER pushed to Vercel production

Cron reliability:
- lapsed-clients: await SMS before writing audit event, rename action to lapsed_client_contacted
- onboarding-drip: log audit event on drip email failures for ops visibility

Data integrity:
- lib/db/costs.ts: getProjectCosts() now takes optional take param (default 500, was unlimited)
- app/api/webhooks/blooio: tighten phone digit validation to regex /^\d{10}$/

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: add C5, mTOR, GAA to ambiguity overrides

These genes are correctly identified but flagged as cross-type conflicts
due to slightly different wording between gene name and abbreviation
long-form. Fuzzy matching (>0.6 ratio) already handles these, but
explicit overrides provide a safety net.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Pull-to-dismiss aircraft info panel from anywhere in content

Swipe down anywhere on the info panel to dismiss it — no need to grab
the handle bar. Activates when content is scrolled to top and finger
moves downward. Panel follows finger in real-time with opacity fade.
Swipe back up mid-gesture to keep it open. Smooth animated dismissal
when dragged past 80px threshold, snaps back otherwise.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
